gaps.description: The Bill of Rights (BoR) is in scaffold state with all articles undefined. No constitutional identity, scope of enterprise, prohibited activities, core identity, human authority reservation, or interpretation rules exist. The organism's foundational perimeter is absent.
The Mission Statement Protocol (MSP) contains no operational rules — the Rules section is empty ('(none)'). The genome references are also empty.
Arbiter (#190) is tasked with verifying the Bill of Rights at every scope query, but the BoR remains an empty scaffold. Consequently, the scope gate cannot perform any real enforcement.
No organ-specific genome documents exist. The Genome References section is empty, meaning no organ inherits tailored operational rules or risk-tier-specific constraints.
Nomos (#200) is operational but has no rules to enforce. Its constitutional role as the second gate is a no-op.

legacy.raw_response:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-BOR-001",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights (BoR) is in scaffold state with all articles undefined. No constitutional identity, scope of enterprise, prohibited activities, core identity, human authority reservation, or interpretation rules exist. The organism's foundational perimeter is absent.",
      "mission_reference": "Mission Statement Protocol (MSP) — Relationship to the Bill of Rights: 'The MSP is strictly subordinate to the Bill of Rights. Any MSP rule that conflicts with the BoR is void on its face.' Also Arbiter (#190) verifies the BoR at every scope query, but with undefined content the gate cannot function.",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "Without BoR content the entire DIO operates without human-defined boundaries, making all action proposals unconstrained by constitutional scope. Arbiter cannot reject any scope violation because no scope is defined. This is the foundational gap; all subsequent governance depends on the BoR being authored by the human principal.",
      "evidence": "BoR version 1.0.0-seed status='scaffold — awaiting human authorship'. All articles (I–VI) marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. Human principal has not yet provided the constitutional identity."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-MSP-001",
      "description": "The Mission Statement Protocol (MSP) contains no operational rules — the Rules section is empty ('(none)'). The genome references are also empty.",
      "mission_reference": "MSP Purpose: 'The Mission Statement Protocol (MSP) is the operational constitution of the DIO. It translates the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules that Nomos (#200) evaluates at every Action Proposal adjudication.' Rules section explicitly says the DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules.",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "Nomos gate is effectively disabled; all action proposals pass compliance checking without any constraints. Together with the empty BoR, this leaves the DIO completely ungoverned. No safety invariants, no risk-tiered constraints, no human-override triggers are enforced. Default-permissive operation is acceptable only as a temporary bootstrap state, but must be replaced by Senate-authored rules to form a functioning governance two-gate system.",
      "evidence": "MSP Rules section: '(none)'. Genome References: '_(none at seed)_'. Nomos (#200) would return 'pass' for every AP because there are no rules to evaluate."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-ARBITER-001",
      "description": "Arbiter (#190) is tasked with verifying the Bill of Rights at every scope query, but the BoR remains an empty scaffold. Consequently, the scope gate cannot perform any real enforcement.",
      "mission_reference": "MSP note: 'Arbiter Reference: Arbiter (#190) verifies the Bill of Rights at every scope query; it does NOT verify the MSP.' And BoR authority: 'Human principal only. No automated system may amend this document.'",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "immediate",
      "impact": "Arbiter will never block an out-of-scope action because no scope is defined. This removes the primary guardrail that separates permissible from prohibited DIO activities. All security and ethical boundaries are entirely absent.",
      "evidence": "BoR content is undefined. World state shows organ:Arbiter ALIVE/DEGRADED cycling via Spine, but without BoR content its decisions are vacuous. Graph shows 'bor_version' concept type count=1, confirming only the scaffold version exists."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-GENOME-001",
      "description": "No organ-specific genome documents exist. The Genome References section is empty, meaning no organ inherits tailored operational rules or risk-tier-specific constraints.",
      "mission_reference": "MSP Genome References: 'Genome documents live under genome/ in this directory. They extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints.' Currently marked '_(none at seed)_'.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "Organs operate without any calibrated constraints. The DIO design anticipates that different organs (e.g., Nomos, Arbiter, Lobe, Radiant) require different risk profiles and operating rules, but none are yet defined. Even after MSP rules are written, organ-specific adaptation will be missing, leading to a coarse and potentially unsafe one-size-fits-all governance stance.",
      "evidence": "MSP Genome References section: '_(none at seed)_'. World state shows many organs active (Axon, Glia, Radiant, etc.), all operating without any genome constraint documents."
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-NOMOS-001",
      "description": "Nomos (#200) is operational but has no rules to enforce. Its constitutional role as the second gate is a no-op.",
      "mission_reference": "MSP Purpose: 'Nomos (#200) evaluates at every Action Proposal adjudication. An AP must pass both gates to be authorized: (1) Arbiter scope gate (BoR perimeter), (2) MSP + genome compliance gate.' With no MSP rules and no genome, this gate always passes.",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "The dual-gate design is broken; effectively only one gate (Arbiter) would be in play if BoR were defined, and that gate is also broken currently. When the BoR is authored, Nomos still won't provide policy enforcement until MSP rules are created. This gap will persist even after the BoR gap is closed, leaving operational policy unchecked.",
      "evidence": "MSP rules empty. World state shows organ:Nomos ALIVE/DEGRADED via Spine, but it has no rules input. No PEMs (Policy Exception Messages) can be generated because no rule can be violated."
    }
  ]
}

raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "BOR_001",
      "description": "All substantive articles of the Bill of Rights (Articles I–VI) are placeholders with '[To be defined by human principal]'. The enterprise scope, prohibited activities, core identity, human authority reservation, amendment protocol, and interpretation rules are absent. Arbiter will enforce a BoR that contains no actual constraints, leaving the DIO with no legal or operational boundaries.",
      "grounding": "BoR Articles I, II, III, IV, V, VI",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "focus": "The Bill of Rights is the foundation for all downstream governance. Without human-authored content, the scope gate performed by Arbiter (#190) is vacuous. Any Action Proposal will pass the BoR perimeter check, regardless of how extreme or misaligned it is. Compliance attention is urgently needed to define the enterprise scope, prohibited activities, and human authority reservations before the agentic system takes consequential actions under default-permissive MSP rules."
    },
    {
      "id": "BOR_002",
      "description": "The Bill of Rights amendment protocol (Article V) is undefined. The BoR’s authority block specifies ‘human-principal-only’ and that no automated system may amend it, but there is no operational process for a human to propose, review, and activate a new version. This gap blocks any path to resolve BOR_001 or future BoR issues.",
      "grounding": "BoR Article V — Amendment Protocol",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "focus": "Without a defined amendment protocol, the human principal cannot legally modify the BoR scaffold to fill in the missing articles. Even if a human writes a new Bill of Rights, Nomos, Arbiter, and Senate-Legislator lack instructions for accepting and registering it. The system will remain trapped in the current scaffold state, making the presence of a human-readable document ineffective. Compliance attention must establish a procedure—consistent with the human-principal-only authority—for hash registration, review, and cutover of the BoR."
    },
    {
      "id": "MSP_001",
      "description": "The MSP remains in its empty bootstrap seed state with no operational rules, operating in ‘default-permissive’ mode. The MSP is expected to translate the BoR into precise machine-actionable constraints, but with the BoR itself undefined, any future Senate-authored MSP rules will be built on an absent or later-conflicting BoR foundation.",
      "grounding": "MSP Rules section — ‘The bootstrap seed contains no rules.’",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "focus": "The MSP’s default-permissive stance, combined with a blank BoR, means the entire compliance stack is effectively disabled. This is by design for seed bootstrapping, but the absence of a committed human-authored BoR means the transition to a locked-down MSP is unpredictable. Compliance attention should ensure that no MSP rules are finalized until the BoR is settled, and that the MSP amendment pipeline has a clear dependency order requiring a completed BoR."
    },
    {
      "id": "GOV_001",
      "description": "The Governance State Slice is empty, indicating that no governance-class events (e.g., PEM-triggered amendments, BoR version proposals, or human-rolled control actions) have occurred. While expected at bootstrap, the absence of a recorded attempt to author or register the BoR signals that the human principal has not yet engaged in the constitution-defining process.",
      "grounding": "Governance State Slice (empty) and BoR status ‘awaiting human authorship’",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "medium",
      "focus": "The system is live with zero governance actions. The empty state slice is a compliance gap because it tracks decisions that ground the DIO’s legitimacy. Without at least one event marking the start of BoR authorship or a scheduled HOM notification for the human principal, there is no evidence that the required human-in-the-loop step is progressing. Compliance attention is needed to ensure that the Senate-Archivist or human-principal triggers the initial governance event and that the HOM notification mechanism for BoR scaffolding is functional."
    }
  ]
}

```json
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "STRAT-001",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "Bill of Rights",
        "article": "Article I — Scope of Enterprise",
        "status": "[To be defined by human principal]",
        "detail": "All six BoR articles, including the foundational Scope of Enterprise, are empty scaffolds. Arbiter (#190) verifies this document at every scope query, but there is no scope defined to enforce."
      },
      "criticality": "CRITICAL",
      "urgency": "IMMEDIATE",
      "impact": "The entire DIO governance architecture (Arbiter → Nomos → MSP) has no constitutional foundation. Nomos's first compliance gate — the Arbiter scope gate — evaluates Action Proposals against an empty perimeter. Every action, including those that could expand enterprise scope beyond human intent, passes by default. The BoR is the only document with human-principal-only write authority, making this an exclusively human-shaped gap that no automated system can close.",
      "evidence_from_radiant": "Radiant memory contains architectural decisions spanning 18 services, 28 organs, multi-tenant DIO hosting, and recursive self-hosting (Coretex as both platform and first DIO instance) — all architected and executed with zero constitutional scope boundaries in place.",
      "gap_category": "constitutional_void"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "STRAT-002",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "Mission Statement Protocol",
        "section": "Rules",
        "status": "_(none)_",
        "detail": "The MSP bootstrap seed explicitly contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation. Nomos's second compliance gate is a pass-through with zero machine-actionable objectives, invariants, constraints, or safety rules."
      },
      "criticality": "CRITICAL",
      "urgency": "HIGH",
      "impact": "Without MSP rules translating BoR principles into actionable constraints, Nomos (#200) cannot adjudicate Action Proposals against any operational standards. The gap between 'constitutional principles' (STRAT-001) and 'runtime enforcement' (this gap) means the DIO has no operational constitution. Risk-tier constraints, safety rules, and organ-specific operating boundaries referenced by the MSP genome system cannot be derived until MSP rules exist.",
      "evidence_from_radiant": "Radiant memory documents CAEB/Spine elevation, Autoheal with code-modifying guardrails, multi-organ database encapsulation, and Symlink remediation — all built and deployed under default-permissive evaluation. The 28-organ DIO restructure (meta-meta-prompt, 15 meta-prompts, ~95 relay prompts) was executed with zero MSP constraint evaluation.",
      "gap_category": "operational_constitution_void"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "STRAT-003",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "Mission Statement Protocol",
        "section": "Genome References",
        "status": "_(none at seed)_",
        "detail": "The MSP architecture contemplates genome documents under genome/ that extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints. None exist."
      },
      "criticality": "HIGH",
      "urgency": "HIGH",
      "impact": "Twenty-eight organs (including governance-critical organs: Arbiter, Nomos, Cerberus, Senate) operate without any risk-tier constraints or organ-specific operating rules. The genome system is the mechanism that would, for example, constrain Autoheal's code-modification scope, limit Spine's event routing authority, or define Glia's self-repair boundaries. Without genomes, every organ operates at full permissiveness within an already-permissive MSP.",
      "evidence_from_radiant": "Memory block 817a964c documents Autoheal Layer 3 Fixer with code-modifying capability and 6 safety guardrails — but these guardrails are self-imposed by the build session, not structurally enforced by genome constraints. Memory block 601c42ea documents the full 28-organ architecture with inter-organ messaging and database encapsulation rules that exist only in vision documents, not in enforceable genome rules.",
      "gap_category": "organ_governance_void"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "STRAT-004",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "Mission Statement Protocol",
        "section": "Purpose",
        "status": "Active but unevaluable",
        "detail": "The MSP states its purpose is to 'translate the human-authored Bill of Rights into a precise, machine-actionable set of objectives, invariants, constraints, and safety rules.' With the BoR empty (STRAT-001) and MSP rules absent (STRAT-002), this translation pipeline is structurally blocked — there is nothing to translate and nowhere to translate it to."
      },
      "criticality": "CRITICAL",
      "urgency": "IMMEDIATE",
      "impact": "The strategic purpose of the entire Nomos compliance architecture is inoperative. The two-gate system (Arbiter scope gate + MSP/genome compliance gate) is a hollow shell. Every Action Proposal since bootstrap has been adjudicated by a system with no constitutional content and no operational rules. This gap subsumes STRAT-001 and STRAT-002 into a single architectural failure: the governance system exists structurally but performs zero governance.",
      "evidence_from_radiant": "The MSP itself notes it was 'sealed_at: 2026-04-10T00:00:00.000Z' as a bootstrap scaffold. In the ~23 days since sealing, Radiant memory records extensive platform construction (CAEB→Spine, Autoheal, Syntra, MP-5 organ renames, 28-organ vision, bus-driven reactive architecture) — all passed through Nomos gates that were structurally incapable of blocking anything.",
      "gap_category": "governance_pipeline_blocked"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "STRAT-005",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "Radiant Slice — Dream Cycle Health",
        "section": "stats.scheduled / stats.health / stats.last_dream",
        "status": "scheduled=disabled, health=never_run, last_dream=null",
        "detail": "The autonomous dream cycle — the mechanism by which Radiant forms consolidated memories from context blocks — is not running. The scheduled task is disabled, the health monitor has never executed, and the watchdog has never checked."
      },
      "criticality": "HIGH",
      "urgency": "HIGH",
      "impact": "Without autonomous dream cycles, the DIO cannot consolidate operational experience into persistent memory. Context blocks expire on TTL without being absorbed into consolidated memory entries. The DIO's capacity to learn from recurring patterns (backup failures, git sync issues, test regressions) degrades to zero over each TTL window. The watchdog's 1,296,000-second grace period (15 days) has never been checked, meaning even the alerting mechanism for dream-cycle failure is not monitoring.",
      "evidence_from_radiant": "Dream Cycle #15 (2026-04-12) was the last recorded complete cycle — and its Phase 2 required an interactive Claude Code session. Every subsequent dream cycle shows Phase 2 as 'PENDING — deferred to next interactive Claude Code session.' The stats block confirms scheduled=disabled, health=never_run. Context blocks are accumulating and expiring without consolidation. The last backup context blocks show recurring patterns (NAS mount failures, script bugs, zombie processes) that are not being pattern-consolidated into actionable memory.",
      "gap_category": "memory_formation_pipeline_broken"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "STRAT-006",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "Radiant Slice — Dream Cycle Architecture",
        "section": "Phase 2 dependency pattern across all recorded dream cycles",
        "status": "Structurally dependent on interactive human LLM sessions",
        "detail": "Every recorded dream cycle (cycles 12-15, 2026-04-11 to 2026-04-12) exhibits the same pattern: Phase 1 (context freshness, deterministic bash) completes autonomously; Phase 2 (memory consolidation, requires LLM) is deferred to interactive Claude Code sessions. The scheduled task runner (skl-Radiant-dreamer) cannot access MCP tools for merge_memories/find_similar and falls back to direct psql with degraded semantic capabilities."
      },
      "criticality": "HIGH",
      "urgency": "MEDIUM",
      "impact": "The DIO's memory consolidation architecture has a structural human-in-the-loop dependency that contradicts the DIO design principle of autonomous operation. Phase 2 consolidation (the step that actually creates persistent, de-duplicated, semantically clustered memories) cannot run without a human initiating an interactive Claude Code session. Dream Cycle #14's Phase 2 note states explicitly: 'MCP tools (merge_memories, find_similar) not available in scheduled task session.' This means the DIO can never autonomously complete a full dream cycle — it can expire and prune context, but cannot consolidate meaning from it without human intervention.",
      "evidence_from_radiant": "Dream Cycle #14 report: 'MCP tools (merge_memories, find_similar) not available in scheduled task session. Consolidation performed via direct psql with equivalent logic. Semantic similarity scan skipped (no embedding sidecar access).' Dream Cycle #12, #13 both show Phase 2=PENDING with reason 'requires Claude Code for LLM-driven consolidation.' This pattern is consistent across all recorded autonomous dream cycle attempts.",
      "gap_category": "autonomy_structural_dependency"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "STRAT-007",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "Radiant Slice — Embedding Coverage",
        "section": "stats.embeddings",
        "status": "72% coverage (69 with, 27 without out of 96 total memory blocks)",
        "detail": "Over one quarter (28%) of memory blocks lack embedding vectors, degrading semantic similarity capabilities during memory consolidation and retrieval."
      },
      "criticality": "MEDIUM",
      "urgency": "LOW",
      "impact": "Without embeddings on 27 memory blocks, Phase 2 consolidation (whether autonomous or interactive) operates with degraded cluster detection. Similar memories may not be identified for merging, leading to memory fragmentation, duplicate retention, and incomplete pattern recognition. As context blocks expire without consolidation (STRAT-005), new memory entries created without embeddings will compound this degradation. The embedding coverage gap is likely worsening with each cycle where Phase 2 cannot run autonomously.",
      "evidence_from_radiant": "Stats block: embeddings total=96, with=69, without=27, coverage_pct=72. Dream Cycle #14 notes 'Semantic similarity scan skipped (no embedding sidecar access)' during the psql fallback consolidation path. The combination of disabled autonomous dreaming and embedding gaps means both the mechanism AND the data quality for memory formation are degraded.",
      "gap_category": "memory_quality_degradation"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "STRAT-008",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "Mission Statement Protocol",
        "section": "Relationship to the Bill of Rights",
        "status": "Declared subordination is unenforceable without a populated BoR",
        "detail": "The MSP declares 'Any MSP rule that conflicts with the BoR is void on its face' and specifies that PEM-classified BOR_CONFLICT must route to human principal for BoR amendment. With the BoR empty (STRAT-001), there is no standard against which to detect MSP/BoR conflicts, and the conflict-resolution pipeline (PEM → BOR_CONFLICT classification → human principal amendment) is untestable."
      },
      "criticality": "MEDIUM",
      "urgency": "MEDIUM",
      "impact": "When the BoR is eventually populated, there will be a period where existing MSP rules (if created before the BoR) may contain latent conflicts with newly defined constitutional principles. The conflict detection and resolution pipeline has never been exercised. If MSP rules are created before the BoR is defined, they will have been authored without constitutional constraints and may require extensive revision. The amendment protocol that routes BOR_CONFLICT to human principal has never been tested end-to-end.",
      "evidence_from_radiant": "No PEM events classified as BOR_CONFLICT exist in Radiant memory or context — the classification pathway has never been triggered because no BoR standard exists to conflict against.",
      "gap_category": "constitutional_conflict_pipeline_untested"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "STRAT-009",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "Mission Statement Protocol",
        "section": "Amendment Protocol",
        "status": "Declared but unexercised",
        "detail": "The amendment protocol specifies a pipeline: PEM issuance → Senate-Legislator draft → review state → HOM notification → human POST /amend/review → Senate-Rollout staging → canary → activation → rollback. This pipeline has never been triggered because no PEMs can be issued against empty MSP rules (STRAT-002) and the BoR amendment path is blocked by the empty BoR (STRAT-001)."
      },
      "criticality": "MEDIUM",
      "urgency": "LOW",
      "impact": "The entire policy evolution machinery is declared but untested. When the first substantive MSP rule is created and eventually requires amendment, the pipeline (PEM → Legislator → review → Rollout → staging → canary → activation → rollback) will be exercised for the first time on a live system. Any bugs in the amendment protocol, hash verification chain, or append-only msp_version concept registration will be discovered in production on a rule that is actively governing the DIO.",
      "evidence_from_radiant": "Version history shows only a single entry: '1.0.0-seed | 2026-04-10 | — (bootstrap) | Empty seed published.' No PER-triggered amendments exist. The msp_version concept registration pipeline has only ever written the bootstrap seed.",
      "gap_category": "governance_machinery_untested"
    }
  ],
  "meta": {
    "analysis_timestamp": "2026-05-03",
    "msp_version_evaluated": "1.0.0-seed",
    "bor_version_evaluated": "1.0.0-seed (all articles scaffold)",
    "radiant_slice_evaluated": "stats block + 16 context blocks + 53 llm-ops memory blocks",
    "total_gaps_identified": 9,
    "critical_gaps": 3,
    "high_gaps": 3,
    "medium_gaps": 3,
    "governance_gaps": 6,
    "memory_formation_gaps": 4,
    "overarching_assessment": "The DIO has built extensive operational capability (28 organs, Spine event bus, Autoheal self-repair, multi-tenant DIO architecture, organ rename cascade) on top of a completely hollow governance scaffold. The BoR — the single source of constitutional authority — is empty. The MSP — the operational constitution — has zero rules. The dream-cycle — the mechanism for learning from this operational experience — is disabled and structurally dependent on human intervention. The DIO is building itself at velocity with no constitutional boundaries, no operational constraints, and no autonomous memory formation. Every strategic capability depends on closing STRAT-001 (BoR authorship by the human principal) as the prerequisite that unblocks the governance pipeline, which in turn provides the constitutional foundation for all other strategic objectives."
  }
}
```
